title: Comforting Dense Ukrainian Beef Dumplings
description: A traditional Ukrainian dish featuring feather blade beef, these dumplings are fermented, dense, and kid-friendly, making them a great low-carb option for dinner.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, chopped onion, eggs, salt, and pepper, and mix well until just combined.
Gradually add the flour to the beef mixture, stirring with a wooden spoon until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of sour cream in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Place the dumplings in a large pot of boiling water and cook for 10 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and place them in a bain-marie to keep warm.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or dill.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
